body_txt: Characters often have Hidden Depths - skills that you wouldn't assume they would possess. But the natural question this raises is "Why hide it?" Surely, something so useful would have come in handy earlier, right? Sure, maybe it just never came up . But in many cases, those depths are hidden for a reason. This character has a useful skill that they hate , and avoid using if at all possible. In many instances where this trope is played straight, the skill in question will be related to hurting or killing other people, which they find distasteful. This is a common trait of the Martial Pacifist , who is an expert fighter but prefers the art or the sport of their trade and dislikes real violence. Even if it's not, the reason they don't like doing it might be that they accidentally hurt or killed someone (maybe even someone they love ) when they did. The skill might even be harmful to the character themself . There are plenty of more innocuous reasons that someone might refuse to do something they're good at, though. One particularly prominent one is that the skill is something embarrassing and they're ashamed of showing it off to others. Taken to its extreme, characters might hide any unusual skill they have out of fear they'll be perceived as different; this often figures in An Aesop about why you should Be Yourself . Another reason is that the skill is very powerful with no downside, but the character worries they'll come to rely on it, or wants to challenge themself. Subtrope of Willfully Weak . Compare Afraid of Their Own Strength , Cursed with Awesome , and The Perils of Being the Best . Contrast What Kind of Lame Power Is Heart, Anyway? and Blessed with Suck . This is a very common reason that a character Doesn't Like Guns . More comedic examples might be introduced right before a Gilligan Cut to the character doing that very thing. The reason this trope comes into play is very often a spoiler, so all spoilers below are unmarked. You Have Been Warned .
